headline.title: FedEx deepens Viettel Post partnership — Vietnam logistics consolidation gains a global anchor
headline.summary: FedEx's expanded tie-up with Viettel Post signals that global integrators view Vietnam as a structural last-mile market, not a tactical one. The move puts pressure on domestic 3PLs and accelerates the formalization of cross-border e-commerce flows through Vietnam.

items.title: China wants its provinces to compete, but not get in each other’s way
items.summary: As Beijing increasingly sees the country as a chessboard, the central government is no longer simply asking every province to grow faster; it wants them to grow differently. Reporting on the end of the 14th five-year plan and preparation for the 15th five-year plan frames provinces and cities as specialised implementation ...

items.title: Prepare for ‘China shock 3.0’ to the global food economy
items.summary: Even as “China shock 2.0” is roiling Western manufacturers, we must, it seems, brace for “China shock 3.0” – to the global food economy – as President Xi Jinping doubles down on the imperative that has obsessed Beijing for decades: food security. “China’s Food Future”, a consultation paper by Systemiq funded by the California-based Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation, warns that China is poised t...

items.title: FedEx bets on Viettel Post to strengthen capabilities in Vietnam
items.summary: FedEx and Viettel Post are expanding a partnership covering last-mile delivery and cross-border parcel handling. The deal positions Viettel Post as the preferred domestic operator for one of the two dominant global integrators in Vietnam, raising the bar for domestic 3PL competitors.
items.raw_path: raw/FedEx bets on Viettel Post to strengthen capabilities in Vietnam.md

items.title: VASEP urges removal of trade barriers to reach USD 12bn seafood export target in 2026
items.summary: Vietnam's seafood industry association is lobbying for streamlined export inspections and tariff relief. The 12bn target implies ~12% YoY growth and depends heavily on shrimp pricing in the US and pangasius demand recovery in China.
items.raw_path: raw/VASEP kiến nghị gỡ rào cản để xuất khẩu thủy sản đạt 12 tỷ USD trong năm 2026.md

items.title: Vietnam FDI: rebalancing toward equitable and sustainable benefits
items.summary: Policy discussion increasingly frames FDI quality (technology transfer, ESG, local-content) over headline volume. A pivot in incentive design could compress margins for legacy assemblers while favoring high-value semiconductor and renewables capex.
items.raw_path: raw/FDI tại Việt Nam Hướng tới lợi ích công bằng và bền vững.md

items.title: Why the world needs China to save more, not less
items.summary: Contrarian read on Chinese household savings: a forced consumption pivot may amplify deflationary spillovers in goods-exporting EM. Implication for Vietnam: continued price competition in textiles, steel, and consumer electronics.
items.raw_path: raw/Why the world needs China to save more, not less.md
